Fade up on a dreamy image of a woman, backlit by the sun.
She walks toward camera with a strange uneven gait, then stops.
Have you seen my other shoe? I'm always losing things.
She looks around for the shoe, then notices something.
She smiles and moves in closer.
BLARING ALARM SOUND.
INT. CALVIN'S BEDROOM. EARLY MORNING.
CALVIN (29) wakes, jolted, alone in bed. A porn DVD menu is on the TV. Calvin turns the TV off with the remote, pulls his sheets away from his body. They are sticky.
EXT. SILVERLAKE STREET.
Calvin stands in his sweats at the side of the road. His body has obviously not seen sunlight for quite some time. Calvin's dog SCOTTY, small and sensitive, sniffs around.
Go potty. Go potty. Go potty. Go potty. Go potty. Go potty. Scotty. potty.
Scotty squats and pees like a girl dog.
INT. CALVIN'S KITCHEN.

LATER. This is a nicer house than we would expect a guy this young to have. Very little furniture, but everything is expensive. Calvin pours coffee. Toast pops up. Scotty waits for scraps. He Loves Me
INT. CALVIN'S OFFICE.

Calvin places the toast and coffee carefully next to the typewriter on his desk. Everything in the room is white: desk, floor, walls. Scotty tries to come in. Calvin shuts the door. No Scotty. Calvin sits. Silence. He positions his hands over the keys. A page sits in the typewriter. Blank. Calvin's face. blank. CUT between the page and Calvin. It's like a staring contest. The phone rings, loud, jarring. Yes? Calvin answers.
INT. GYM. MORNING.
Calvin and Harry are strapped into weight machines that bear more than a passing similarity to Medieval torture devices. Harry stands next to Calvin, spotting him.
Great way to start the day, right?
I think I'm going to throw up.
How was your date last week?
Okay as in you got laid? Harry.
He gestures at a woman working out next to them, earbuds in.
The only reason girls ever want to sleep with me is that they read my book in high school. So?
So they're not interested in me, they're interested in...some idea of me.
That's why you have to work out. they'll want you for your body.
(RE: the machine) What does this thing even do?
It turns you into a god.
...I had a weird dream last night. There was this girl--
What'd she look like?
...like a normal girl, someone I made up.
Did you have sex?
No, she just talked to me.
That's fucking depressing.
It was actually really nice.
Seriously? You don't even get laid in your dreams? That's just sad. (He finishes his reps.) So how's the book coming?
INT. THERAPIST'S OFFICE.
LATE AFTERNOON.
CLOSE UP on Calvin's face, looking up at camera.

I don't know. I don't know. I get an idea, a good idea, like, why don't I write about my dad? And BAM, I start thinking that is the stupidest thing ever, who wants to read about "He was disappointed in me, bla bla bla." But then I'm sitting there, alone, in front of my typewriter all day, and maybe I get something to eat, and all I can think about is how every second I'm getting older and he was right, what am I doing with my life? And also. Also I am feeling ambivalent about Scotty. A beat.
We hold on Calvin.

It's true. I'm feeling very ambivalent about Scotty. Dogs can be really annoying. He slobbers. He chews things. He pees like a girl, which for some reason makes me feel inadequate. But then I feel bad, because if I don't love him, who will? (Pause) Plus he needs to go outside a lot, and it breaks up my day. Maybe that's why I'm not writing?
Smart, fifties. He looks bemused.
Do you think that's why you're not writing? Calvin has somehow managed to squish his body into a fetal position in the chair. No.
Why do you think you're not writing?
...Can I have Bobby now?
Do you need Bobby now? Yes.
Dr. Rosenthal gets up and goes to a cabinet. him with his eyes.
friend?
Calvin follows
When was the last time you saw a
Harry, yesterday.
Someone other than your brother?
Calvin doesn't answer. Dr. Rosenthal comes over and hands him a plush teddy bear: Bobby. Calvin holds it. Dr. Rosenthal goes back to his seat.
How was your date Tuesday?
Calvin plays with Bobby's ears.

I don't know that I'm really a dating kind of person. Just the prospect of like...childhood stories, relationship histories, "You're depressed? Oh my god I'm depressed"... Besides, if I haven't written that day, that's all I can think about. Have you been giving Bobby to your other patients?
No, Bobby's just for you.
'Cause he smells weird.
Calvin. When you were deciding to get Scotty, what did we talk about? What did you say you hoped for? Do you remember? Calvin mutters something.
A little louder please.
That he would be awesome and we'd take hikes and stuff. And I would meet people when they stopped to pet him. But Scotty
gets scared when people try to pet him.
Does that embarrass you? No.
(Big fat lie)
...I want to give you a writing assignment.
I can't write.

Okay. This would just be for me. I'd like you to write, just a page, about someone who sees Scotty, all slobbery and scared, and likes him anyway. Just the way he is. Do you think you could do that for me? Beat.
Can it be bad?
I would like it to be very bad.
INT. HAMMER MUSEUM. EVENING.
CLOSE ON: Scattered hardcover books surround A BLOWN UP POSTER OF A BOOK COVER propped on a table. It reads: HEARTBROKEN OLD TIMES By Calvin Weir-Fields
The first time I met Calvin, he was 19 years old and had already been at the top of the New York Times Best Seller List for months.
A crowd of well-dressed people sit watching: LANGDON THARP, literary god. Late forties and enjoying the attention from the stage.
I thought: who the fuck is this kid? And how can I go back in time and be him? Laughter.
Calvin stands in the wings.
This skinny high school drop out with acne still on his chin gave us what very well may become a classic American novel.
Someone pats Calvin on the shoulder.

Since then, we've had the pleasure of reading his short stories, and of course last year's novella "Breakfast for Dinner." But it wasn't until I sat down to re-read his novel-(Holding up the book) --in this beautiful new edition--that I remembered quite how good Calvin WeirFields is.
Langdon turns, a strange edge to his voice.
Ten years ago or ten years from now, we're lucky to have him around. Ladies and Gentlemen, Calvin Weir-Fields.
People applaud. Calvin walks onstage. Langdon shakes his hand. Flashbulbs. Calvin takes the mic, ready to speak.
INT. MUSEUM BATHROOM. LATER.

Calvin splashes water on his face. Water drips off his hair, nose, eyelashes. He looks like a drowning man. INT. MUSEUM. HALL. JUST AFTER. Calvin walks down the wall. Cyrus pops in from around the corner. Cyrus is either a good guy who comes off as a douche, or a douche who gives phenomenal "good-guy." No one knows.
There's the man!
You have to remind me about these things.
Check your messages buddy.
I would have at least worn a real shirt.
No one cares what you wear, you're a genius.
Don't use that word.
INT. MUSEUM AUDITORIUM. JUST AFTER.
Calvin makes his way up the stairs out of the auditorium. People press forward to speak to him. Phenomenal, man. Just brilliant. Brilliant.
Thanks for coming. Hey, man, you know that chapter where Charlie goes to the whorehouse? Did you
make the whore's dress blue because it was the same color as his mother's apron?
...Sorry, what?
A SORT OF WEIRD GUY cuts in, a little too close to Calvin. Calvin. Calvin. Calvin. So. How did you know where to send your manuscript?
Uh, I looked it up.
On the internet. (CONTINUED) But you were a teenager. Is it weird for you that you used to be so successful?
INT. MUSEUM. RECEPTION AREA (BY BILLY WILDER PORTRAIT).
Langdon pulls Calvin into a group of writers for a photo.
Say "Disillusionment." No one smiles.
Flashbulbs go off. Cyrus steps in.
Hey, Cyrus Modi, huge fan.
INT. MUSEUM. RECEPTION AREA. LATER.
The crowd mills about. The Sort Of Weird Guy chats up Calvin. Cyrus interrupts.
Look, pink litchi-tinis!
I don't want to hold this.
I know it looks gay. Try holding two of them. Now go, sign copies, sell books.
I was thinking of heading home.
Cyrus laughs. Party is more intense. A CUTE HIPSTER GIRL tries to chat up Calvin at the bar. They both have to shout over the noise. Mabel!
CUTE HIPSTER GIRL I'm Mabel! CUTE HIPSTER GIRL
Do I know you?
(Holding out a scrap of paper) No, do you want my number? LATER.
ELEVATOR FOYER.
Calvin is sandwiched between the Coordinator and MICHAEL SILVERBLATT, talking intently into his ear. They head into the bookstore.
You know my show Bookworm--?
Yeah, of course I--
Well, I have to tell you I love the first book, but what we really want is for you to come on the show and talk about what you're working on now, NOW is the question--
Langdon chats up some women.
Of course, Calvin always had talent, but that will only get you so far. I helped hone him, give him some style. It was a passing of the baton, really.
Mabel continues to hit on Calvin as he signs her book.
My favorite was that story you had in McSweeny's last fall? About the guy trying to buy a jacket?
Langdon chats up some more women.
Everyone sits at home and thinks, "I could do that, a monkey could do that." But one's muse is incredibly difficult. You have to seduce her, finesse her.
A tiny intimidating older FEMALE PROFESSOR intensely grips Calvin's arm.

At first I resisted teaching your book, but the longer I considered it, the more I saw its corollary to Roth and Mailer, so I thought, if I'm going to include anyone contemporary, it really must be Calvin Weir-Fields--
Langdon chats up even more women.
People think of writing as cerebral, but its actually an incredibly physical, almost sensual act. You should see me write. I'm like an Olympian. With a pen.
Mabel's kneeling by the table as Calvin signs other people's books. She has a drink.

I was so in love with Charlie. He was like my ideal in high school. I mean I know he's not real. But he kind of is, right? Like, your name starts with "C" and so does Charlie... I mean, it's like autobiographical, right?
Quieter. Langdon leans against a wall by Calvin. Drunk.
They love you and then they throw you away. It's easier if you've only ever been mediocre. When you've been to the top, it can kill you. (MORE)
Salinger had the right idea: write what you can, and then DISAPPEAR.
BLACK. SILENT. A door opens.
INT. CALVIN'S HOUSE.
NIGHT. Calvin's silhouette in the doorway. He tosses his copy of the book on the floor. Shuts the door behind him.
INT. CALVIN'S BEDROOM. JUST AFTER.

The door opens revealing Scotty asleep on the floor by the bed. There are many torn up books around him. Also a puddle of urine. Calvin examines the remains of the books. One of them was his copy of "CATCHER IN THE RYE." Calvin lets it drop from his fingers in defeat or exhaustion.
INT. CALVIN'S LIVINGROOM.
JUST AFTER. Calvin throws his pillow and comforter on the couch and collapses on top of them. He wraps himself in the comforter and switches off the light. She's so cute.
EXT. PARK. DAY.
Calvin is sitting on the grass, a book open in front of him. Standing a few feet away is the GIRL from his dream. She holds a sketch pad and wears a summer dress. Your dog.
She's so cute.
Scotty trots over and sits by Calvin.
He's a boy dog.
He just peed like a girl.
Have we met before? I don't think so.
I wouldn't get too close. scared of people.
He's a little
Do you mind if I draw him? Go ahead.
She sits cross-legged on the grass and starts to draw.
You're an artist? Yeah. I'm super good. name? Scotty.
What's your dog's
No, I'm half Lithuanian. Is he Scottish? I named him for F. Scott Fitzgerald.
The girl looks up from her drawing.
F. Scott Fitzgerald. Great Gatsby"?
"The (Going back to her drawing) I don't read a lot of fiction. You've never heard of F. Scott Fitzgerald. (CONTINUED)
He Loves Me

Why, is he really famous and important? He's probably one of the greatest novelists who ever lived. How do you know? What? (Looking up) How do you know he's one of the greatest novelists who ever lived? Have you read every novel ever written? (Super annoyed) ...No. But I am a novelist. I know a lot about novels. And Fitzgerald is pretty much the best. Okay. (Going back to her sketch) But I don't think you like him very much. What? Naming your dog after him? disrespectful.
It's a little

It's a gesture. Yeah, an aggressive gesture. Think about it: you're a novelist, you think this guy is the greatest, you name your dog after him to cut him down to size. Now you can put him on a leash and yell "Bad Scotty" and feel all superior 'cuz you pee inside. Kill your idols, man, I'm all for it.
Calvin is annoyed. He gets up to go.
GIRL (CONT'D)
She rips off the page and hands it to him. It's an incredibly beautiful sketch of Scotty. Calvin looks at her.
This is beautiful.
What did you say? I like him. Just the way he is.
INT. CALVIN'S LIVNGROOM. DAY.
Calvin sits straight up on the couch.
The sound of type-writer typing rapidly over:
INT. CALVIN'S HOUSE. DAY AND NIGHT.

Images in super fast motion: Calvin writes. Scotty's tail thumps. Calvin revises with whiteout. Scotty runs in with a HIGH HEELED SHOE in his mouth. Calvin looks at it weird, then throws it for Scotty. Scotty chews on the high heel. Calvin types like a mad person. The "ding!" of the typewriter ends the sequence.
INT. THERAPIST'S OFFICE.
ONE WEEK LATER.
You are a genius!!!!
Calvin stands, arms held above his head in triumph. Rosenthal looks pleased with himself.
I thought we weren't going to use that word, Calvin.
Calvin sits. Still happy.
Okay: you are really really really super fucking smart. "I'd like it to be bad." Can I tell you what it's about? Maybe I shouldn't, it could kill the... Fuck it, it's amazing, can I tell you?
Please. DR. ROSENTHAL

Okay: there's this guy, this neurotic writer guy, and he's all lonely, but then he meets this girl, and at first he doesn't like her, but then it turns out... (Hearing himself, lamely) She's perfect for him. It's way less trite than it sounds.
I'm glad you found something that inspires you.

Inspires me? It overwhelms me. I literally cannot sleep or eat. All I want to do is write. I almost didn't come here today, because I didn't want to be away from... (A terrible realization) Oh god. Oh god. What?
I can't say it out loud, it's too stupid.
I love it when you say stupid things.
This is really profoundly stupid. Okay.
Soooo...The guy I'm writing?
What's his name? (CONTINUED)
He Loves Me
Calvin. I'm going to change it. Anyway, there's a lot of me in him? And he's falling for this girl... WHAT I'M TRYING TO SAY IS: It's almost like I'm writing to spend time with her. Who?
The girl. The one I'm writing. I go to sleep at night waiting to get to my typewriter again so I can be with her. It's like... (Whispers) It's almost like I'm in love with her.
(Whispers) That's wonderful.
No. It's not. I can't be in love with the girl I'm writing. Why not?
She's not real. Isn't she? No.
CALVIN
Are you sure?
YES I'M SURE!!!! SHE'S A MOTHER FUCKING PRODUCT OF MY IMAGINATION!!
Calvin buries his head in the seat cushions. Dr. Rosenthal waits.
Lila treated me so badly. I know.
Who leaves someone right after their father dies?
Someone who couldn't love you properly.
Someone who is a heartless cunt. Okay.
Calvin remains supine. Dr. Rosenthal looks at him.
Tell me about her.
I don't want to talk about Lila anymore.
I meant this girl you're writing. me about her.
Calvin looks at Dr. Rosenthal, full of feeling. Ruby.
DREAMY SONG kicks in, over:
EXT. PARK. DAY.
Close up on the Girl's backside. She's roller skating.
Ruby Sparks. Twenty six years old. Raised in Dayton, Ohio.
Why Dayton?
It sounds romantic.
INT. THERAPIST'S OFFICE.
Calvin's face as he summons these thoughts.
Ruby's first crushes were Humphrey Bogart and John Lennon. She cried when she found out they were already dead.
Images of Bogart, Lennon, Ruby as a child. Teenage Ruby (nose ring, dyed black hair) clings to the back of some dude on a motorcycle.
She got kicked out of high school for sleeping with her Art teacher. CALVIN (V.O.) (CONT'D) Or maybe her Spanish teacher. CALVIN (V.O.) (CONT'D) I haven't decided yet.
EXT. BUS STOP. EVENING.
Ruby waits for the bus wearing a restaurant uniform. carries many bags of groceries.
Ruby can't drive. She doesn't own a computer.
INT. RUBY'S APARTMENT.
PRESENT. Ruby's apartment looks like a bomb went off. couch, eating pizza and watching South Park. She sits on the
Ruby hates her middle name, which is Tiffany. She always always roots for the underdog.
INT. THERAPIST'S OFFICE.
Calvin is lost in his story. Ruby's not so good at life sometimes. She forgets to open bills or cash checks.
INT. RUBY'S APARTMENT.
PRESENT. Ruby tries on a dress. the floor. Mounds of clothes lay discarded on
She used to have an eating disorder, but
she doesn't tell anyone about it.
EXT. PARK. DAY.
Ruby bikes ahead. The camera tries to keep up.
Her last boyfriend was forty-nine. The one before that was an alcoholic. She can feel a change coming. She's looking for it.
The camera stops trying to follow her. distance, getting smaller and smaller.
Looking for what?
Ruby bikes into the
Something...new.

Ruby disappears over a hill. DREAMY SONG fades out. Hold on the empty path for a moment. The sound of someone panting. Calvin enters frame near camera, running. He stops by a tree to catch his breath. He calls to someone behind him.
Let's stop for a second.
Harry enters frame at a jog, with Scotty on leash. He slows to a stop and waits for his brother to catch his breath.
INT. CALVIN'S HOUSE.
LATER. Calvin and Harry enter, still in their sweaty jogging clothes, carrying a small bag of groceries.
(Calling out) They didn't have paprika, so I got cumin. That's basically the same, right?
Harry's wife SUSIE (mid-thirties, ethnic) pops out of the kitchen, carrying their baby, MILES.
You have to take Miles, he won't stop grabbing my hair. (To Calvin) Callie, you better thank me because I cleaned your oven.
Oh my woman...
He wraps himself around her. Susie laughs, pushing him away.
(Headed upstairs) Thank you Susie.
INT. CALVIN'S BATHROOM. LATER.
Calvin, wet from the shower, dries his hair with a towel. He looks in the mirror, decides to shave. Opens the cabinet, and sees: a pink Venus razor and Venus shaving cream. He inspects it. Weird.
INT. CALVIN'S LIVINGROOM.
JUST AFTER. Calvin, dressed, comes downstairs carrying the razor and shaving cream. Harry is on the couch playing with Miles and watching football. Susie's setting the table.
Um, Susie, did you happen to-HARRY Dude, you didn't tell me you were getting laid. Harry.
I'm just saying: he claims to be writing too much to haul his ass to our house, but it turns out he's just getting too much poon.
I swear to god Harry I am going to duct tape your mouth shut.
I'm not getting any poon.
Harry, eyes still on the game, holds up a hot pink lacy bra.
Who's is that?
You tell me.
(Realizes) Oh god Scotty.
It's Scotty's?
(Taking the bra from Harry and examining it) No, asshole. Scotty's been dragging stuff in. I think he goes through my weird neighbor's garbage.
So that's some random dirty bra? Gross.
Calvin walks to the hall, opens a drawer and drops the bra in.
Calvin! Don't put it away. Throw it away. (Going to the drawer)
I swear, if your mother knew how you boysShe stops when she sees the content of the drawer. Calvin.
What is this?
What is what?
Apparently, Calvin has been collecting women's things. What?!
Leave it--don't--

Harry comes over and looks in the drawer. We see: Apparently Calvin has been collecting women's things. In addition to the bra, there's a pair of panties, a stocking, a brush, and the high heel Scotty brought in. Fuck.
Off.
I swear to god, Scotty's been bringing them in. Right.
"Scotty."
Throw.
INT. CALVIN'S KITCHEN.
These. Away.
EVENING.
Calvin puts the last of the dishes away. In the living room, Susie feeds Miles while watching a movie. Calvin comes over. She looks up at him and smiles.
EXT. CALVIN'S YARD.

JUST AFTER. Calvin approaches Harry, who is sprawled out on a lounge chair reading a stack of pages. Calvin sits at his feet. They are illuminated by the blue light of the pool. Harry puts down the last page. So?
...Where do you see this going?
What do you mean? What are you writing about? I don't know, I just started.
This is a love story, right? You know who reads love stories? Women. And I'm telling you, no woman is going to want to read this.
Fuck you, it's romantic.
Quirky messy women whose problems only make them more endearing are Not. Real. Period. What do they say, write what you been through?
(Muttered) Write what you know. Exactly.
Write what you know.
I've known girls like Ruby. Yeah? Girls. Lila?
Who? HARRY
Not fucking Lila. Right. You've had one relationship-CALVIN For five years--
You never even lived together. I'm telling you, this honeymoon shit doesn't last.
I know that.

Women are different up close. I love Susie, but she's a weirdo. She smells terrible when she's anxious, and sometimes she gets mean as fuck for no reason. She's a person. You haven't written a person, you've written a girl.
(Suddenly angry) Whatever. Most writers don't let people read their work at this stage. I might not even finish it. Silence.
Calvin picks up the manuscript.
Don't, like, tell people about it, okay? Don't tell Susie. Or Mom.
Harry puts a hand on Calvin's shoulder.
I'm not saying you can't write. You could write since you could hold a crayon. I'm saying you don't know jack shit about girls.
Calvin cracks the smallest possible smile.
EXT. CALVIN'S HOUSE.
Harry starts the motor as Susie gets in the car.
Don't let those leftovers go to waste, okay skinny?
Harry leans out the window.
Golf tomorrow?
I'm seeing Cyrus. (He softens.) Maybe Friday.
Harry smiles, pulls away. Calvin watches them go.
INT. CALVIN'S KITCHEN. LATER.
Calvin stands in front of the "women's things" drawer. holds up the bra. Examines it, quizzical.
INT. CALVIN'S OFFICE.
Calvin's hands on the typewriter keys, tapping out words. CLOSE ON his face. His mouth moves as he writes.
This thing is dripping everywhere.
POOL. Calvin and Ruby eat burgers and fries by the pool. tucking a napkin in his shirt. Ruby watches him. He is
How are you supposed to eat these?
Ruby laughs.
You're so not my type.
What do you mean, I'm not your type?
I just usually go for guys who are more... Assertive.
I'm assertive.
You're stubborn. That's different. (Eats a fry) I had this one boyfriend who told me I wasn't funny, but that I had a good sense of humor. Because I laughed at his jokes.
Why would you date a guy like that?
I was young. You must have things you regret. Nope. Mmm.
Everything's been perfect so far.
So is that what you look for in a guy? Douchiness?
No. I was looking for you. I just didn't find you for a while.
Calvin leans in to kiss her. She jumps up, runs, and leaps into the pool, fully clothed.
A spash as she hits the water. Calvin stands and dives in after her. Bodies under water. Over, we hear:
I thought you were the prettiest girl I ever saw.
Were you disappointed? know me? When you got to
How can you ask that? RUBY (V.O.) I'm such a mess.
I love your mess.
RUBY (V.O.) You know what I thought the first time I saw you? I thought: Look at that beautiful boy. I'm going to love him forever and ever and ever.
What if you get sick of me? I won't.
I promise.
INT. CALVIN'S OFFICE.
Calvin sits, looking at the words he's typed. He closes his eyes. "I promise."
INT. CALVIN'S OFFICE.
MORNING. Calvin wakes with a start. Drooling onto his typewriter. The phone is ringing and his cell phone alarm is going off. Shiiiiit. He looks at the time.
Shit shit shit shit shit shit shit. The phone is still ringing. answer it. Hello?
Calvin suddenly remembers to
Just missed the call. He jumps up, dialing his cellphone as he rushes into his bedroom.
Pick up pick up pick up pick-Hell-o. Cyrus!
Where the fuck are you, buddy?
I'm sorry, I was writing and I--
He throws the cell on the bed so he can dress as he talks.
(Shouting at the phone) I was writing and I fell asleep at my desk! I will be right there!
Calvin picks up his cell, grabs his wallet and keys.
If you aren't ready to talk about it, we can always do this another--
Calvin grabs the pages of the manuscript off his desk.
No, I want to show you what I have.
Calvin runs down the stairs.
Give me fifteen minutes, if there isn't any traffic, I should-Scotty is sitting in front of the door. Fuck.
Scotty. CYRUS (O.S.)
Calvin grabs Scotty's collar and reverses direction.
Nothing. My dog needs to pee, it's fine,
I'll leave him in the yard-RUBY (O.C.)
Calvin reverses direction toward the door again, then does a MASSIVE DOUBLE TAKE. Standing in the kitchen, wearing nothing but Calvin's tee and her undies, is RUBY. She is eating cereal. He Loves Me
Calvin stands, gape mouthed, looking at her.
I missed you in bed last night.
You get much writing done? Hello?
Ruby watches him curiously, chomping on cereal.
Calvin hangs up. Stares at Ruby. She holds out her spoon.
Want a bite? It's Crispex.
Calvin sits down, puts his head in his hands.
Oh god.
Calvin?
Are you okay?
It's happening, it's really happening this time. What?
What's wrong?
They're going hospitalize me, they're going to put me in manacles and lobotomize me. They all thought I was sooo smart. Turns out I'm just bat shit-Hey--
She touches his shoulder. up his butt. He jumps like someone put a finger
What the fuck!!!!!!!!!!
He Loves Me
Jesus Christ!!!!!! alone!!
She storms out of the room. Fine, I'll leave you Calvin freaks out to himself.
It's not real it's not real, it's all in my head, I'm dreaming. I'm dreaming, and I'm going to wake up right...NOW.
He opens his eyes. No one is there. Scotty sits there panting happily. Everything's normal. Then: Calvin?
RUBY (O.S.)
Calvin turns. Ruby is standing in the doorway. like a little girl. She looks
Are you mad at me?
INT. KITCHEN.
JUST AFTER. Calvin is on the phone, leaving a message. His back is turned to Ruby, who is cracking eggs into a bowl.
Dr. Rosenthal, it's Calvin Weir-Fields. Something has, um, come up, and I'd appreciate it if you called me?
Calvin checks behind him. Ruby finds a whisk.
(Finishing his message) Um. As soon as possible. He hangs up.
Ruby beats the eggs. Calvin stares at her.
Hey, I was thinking of making these pretty spicy. Is that okay?
Calvin slaps his own face. Calvin? Ruby looks alarmed. Calvin shakes his head vigorously.
She's still there.
What's wrong?
Nothing, nothing, everything. by any chance, yours?
Are these, He pulls a handful of girlie stuff out of the drawer.
Yeah, of course they are, who else's would they be? (Turning pale) Oh god. Are you...are you seeing someone else?
Calvin laughs, a little hysterical.
No. No. I'm not...seeing anyone. Other than you.
He laughs again, more hysterical. Ruby looks worried.
Will you excuse me?
INT. CALVIN'S OFFICE.
JUST AFTER. Close on the phone sitting on the desk.
Remember how Dad used to say I had an "over-active imagination"?
We travel down the phone cord to find Calvin, hiding under his desk, cradling the receiver.

I started seeing her this morning. It's like that movie "Harvey." Except she's not a giant rabbit. It's not like she knows she's imaginary, either. She thinks we're in the relationship in my book. It is freaking me out.
HARRY'S OFFICE.
Harry, in the middle of a very important-looking meeting. Calvin, I'm in the middle of a meeting.
But this is an emergency. losing my mind.
Look. There is no possible way that "Ruby" is in your house, because she is not a real person.

Off leash, Scotty roams ahead of Calvin. Calvin takes in the beauty of the day. Suddenly, Scotty takes off running. Scotty!
Scotty bee-lines over to a girl on a blanket, reading in the sun. She laughs and pets him. Calvin comes over.
I'm so sorry, I--
Calvin stops. It's Ruby. Her hair is shorter, but it's her. She looks up at Calvin...and smiles. Blank. It's fine.
He's so friendly.
She pets Scotty. Calvin crouches down, searching her face, his heart racing. It's definitely her...isn't it?
What's his name? ...Scotty.
That's funny, that's the name of the dog in this book.
She points to her book: "The Girlfriend." Calvin picks it up.
Have you read it?
Calvin nods, head reeling. He absently flips through pages.
Did you like it?
Calvin's eye catches: As soon as she left the house, the past released her. He looks at the girl in front of him.
I'm sorry, did you say something?
I asked if you liked it.
The book.
What do you think of it?
Well, my friend who lent it to me thought it was kind of pretentious, but I like it so far. (Suddenly) Have we met before?
I don't know.
You seem so familiar.
She looks at him searchingly. He watches, waiting for a glimmer of recognition. She smiles.
Maybe we knew each other in another life. Or maybe we just go to the same coffee shop.
She laughs, self-conscious.
What do you do, besides go for walks with your dog?
I'm a writer.
Oh, what do you write?
He holds up the book. bright red. His author photograph. She turns
So that's why you look familiar. CALVIN Maybe.
I was totally kidding about my friend calling it pretentious. (A beat.) Can we start over? ...Yes.
Do you mind if I sit here?
She picks up the book to make room for him.
Just don't tell me how it ends, okay? I promise.
They continue to talk as the camera pulls back, revealing the park in full bloom around them. Credits roll. The End.
